title: Sarcochilus serriformis
taxonomy: Sarcochilus serriformis is a species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is classified under the genus Sarcochilus, which is a small group of epiphytic orchids native to Australia. The classification of Sarcochilus serriformis is further divided into the subfamily Orchidoideae and the tribe Diurideae. Within the tribe Diurideae, Sarcochilus serriformis is placed in the subtribe Diurideinae. This classification helps to identify and understand the unique characteristics of this orchid species.

intro: Sarcochilus serriformis, also known as the Serrate Leafed Pansy Orchid, is a rare and endemic species of orchid found in the tropical rainforests of Australia. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of this plant have been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Sarcochilus serriformis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Sarcochilus serriformis is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in combination with other herbs to create traditional remedies for respiratory and inflammatory conditions.
